title: L-function Comparative Study: Route B vs Route C Discrimination
question: Does the log-prime DFT signal appear in Dirichlet L-function zeros? Does it depend on the arithmetic of the specific L-function (Route B) or reflect GUE universality (Route C)?

chi4.description: Dirichlet L-function L(s, chi_4), conductor 4, odd character

chi3.description: Dirichlet L-function L(s, chi_3), conductor 3, odd character

method.zero_computation: Hardy Z-function approach: Z_chi(t) = exp(i*theta_chi(t)) * L(0.5+it, chi), sign changes located via bisection (mpmath dps=15)
method.snr_computation: Phase 13A method: DFT at omega=log(p) for 9 primes, heights = zero positions, noise = mean DFT power at 8 control frequencies between prime frequencies
method.precision: mp.dps = 15 (lower than Phases 13A/14B due to computational constraints)

N_scaling_RH.description: SNR at key primes vs N zeros used — shows signal emergence threshold

route_B_predictions.description: Route B (explicit formula) predicts detected primes = primes in Euler product. Ramified primes (chi(p)=0) are absent from Euler product and should be ABSENT from detected set.

p2_in_chi4.caveat: chi4 SNR is at noise floor; cannot distinguish true absence from insufficient N

p3_in_chi3.caveat: both SNRs near noise floor; comparison unreliable

p7_unramified_control.prediction: PRESENT in both (Route B and C)
p7_unramified_control.note: chi3 p7 is low — consistent with insufficient N, not predicted absence

verdict.reason: chi4 and chi3 have only 117-132 zeros. RH needs N~200 before log-prime signal emerges above SNR=2.0. At N=130, chi4/chi3 SNRs are all at noise floor (max 1.98). Cannot evaluate Route B vs C at current sample size.
what_we_can_say: The ramified prime p=2 is dramatically suppressed in chi4 (SNR=0.037) vs RH (SNR=4.3) at N=132. This is consistent with Route B.
The p=3 ramified prime in chi3 is also suppressed vs RH. Also consistent with Route B.
Unramified primes (p=7) show comparable or higher SNR in chi4 vs chi3/RH at N~100-130.
These patterns are CONSISTENT with Route B but all SNRs are at noise floor — could be statistical noise.
what_we_cannot_say: Cannot confirm chi4/chi3 would show the log-prime signal at larger N
Cannot confirm ramified prime suppression is arithmetic vs noise
Cannot rule out Route C — GUE synthetics also show flat SNR at N=130
verdict.required_for_conclusive_test: ~400 zeros per L-function (t~1500 for chi4, t~1300 for chi3). Computing this takes ~20-30 min with current mpmath approach. Alternatively: access published LMFDB zero tables (blocked in current environment).
verdict.route_preference: Weakly consistent with Route B based on ramified prime suppression, but inconclusive. Route B remains favored from Phase 13A/15A evidence (GUE synthetics show no log-prime signal).

theoretical_sharpening.key_prediction_for_phase_16b: If Route B is correct: chi4 at N=400+ will show log-prime signal for p=3,5,7,11,13,17,19,23 but NOT p=2. chi3 at N=400+ will show signal for p=2,5,7,11,13,17,19,23 but NOT p=3. The missing prime for each character is its ramified prime — a sharp arithmetic prediction with no free parameters.
theoretical_sharpening.this_is_falsifiable: If at N=400, chi4 shows p=2 signal with SNR > 5, Route B is falsified. If p=2 remains suppressed while p=3..23 emerge, Route B is strongly confirmed.
theoretical_sharpening.katz_sarnak_note: chi4 and chi3 are both primitive characters with unitary symmetry class — same as zeta. So Katz-Sarnak predicts identical GUE statistics. Any difference in which primes are detected must therefore be arithmetic, not statistical.
